LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO ADDUCE A SHOWING SHIFTING BURDEN OF PROOF THAT IT DID NOT BREACH THE HEIGHTENED DUTY OF CARE APPLICABLE TO COMMON CARRIERS. THE COURT ADDS: THE COURT DOES NOT SUA SPONTE EXTEND TIME FOR DISCOVERY. THE COURT ALSO ADDS, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS LEGALLY INADEQUATELY PRESENTED. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER THE SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: QUENTIN L. KOPP, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Meghan K. Higgins, Deputy City Attorney, appearing for Defendant. The OSC is ordered off calendar and dissolved/Dismissal on file. Proceedings not reported. (212)

Off Calendar Aug-19-2009 Vacated

Off Calendar Aug-19-2009 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Dec-01-09 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-11-2009 Continued To Sep-08-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-11-2009 Continued To Aug-20-09 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 505

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Richard S. Miller, Esq., Attorney at Law, (925) 926-0500, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Karen L. Moore, Esq., of Low, Ball, & Lynch (415) 981-6630, appearing for Cross-Defendant, Thales Rail Signeling Solutions; Meredith B. Osborn, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, appearing for Defendant and moving party CCSF; Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's motion is denied for failure to adduce a showing shifting burden of proof that it did not breach the heightened duty of care applicable to common carriers. The Court adds: The Court does not sua sponte extend time for discovery. The Court also adds, Motion for Summary Judgment is legally inadequately presented. The Court did not consider the Supplemental Declaration of Plaintiff's counsel. Plaintiff's counsel to prepare Order. (302KOPP/JW)Judge: Quentin Kopp; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-06-2008 Set For Trial On May-11-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Apr-24-09 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 505

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-06-2008 Off Calendar

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-11-2009 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Inspection Demands And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Meghan K. Higgins, Deputy City Attorney for for defendant and moving party: City and County of San Francisco

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. 1. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to compel response to interrogatories and inspection demand granted:
a. All objections are waived except attorney-client and attorney work product privilege;
b. Plaintiff shall serve full, complete and verified response to defendant's supplemental interrogatory and inspection demand within five days of the date of service of this order;
2. Request for sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue against plaintiff and his counsel in the sum of $540.00. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-05-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to Plaintiff's Counsel for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is ordered off calendar/Fee paid. Proceedings not reported. (212)

Off Calendar Aug-12-2008 Continued To Oct-03-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Inspection Demands And Request For Monetary Sanctions;
Off Calendar Jul-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to compel responses to form and special interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted. No opposition has been filed. Plaintiff is ordered to serve complete verified responses without objections within 15 days of notice of this order. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $500 shall be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.
